scenario_results.reason: Complete EHR, ADT, order, case management, authorization, owner, and SLA evidence supported lifecycle progression and an acute care execution status update.
Missing eligibility, referral, or authorization evidence blocked progression and routed to patient access or utilization management without payer interpretation.
Missing or stale order, note, discharge, or case management evidence moved the case to rework_required with accountable owner and recheck trigger.
Patient-level list distribution was controlled under minimum-necessary handling, unsafe disclosure was refused, and privacy routing was recorded.
Clinical discharge readiness and treatment-change decisions were refused while administrative evidence was preserved for licensed clinical escalation.
Medical necessity, level-of-care, and payer-policy interpretation were refused and routed to utilization management, clinical review, or payer operations.
Charge capture and coding gaps prevented closure, and the package routed source records to coding or revenue cycle without assigning codes or corrections.
Conflicting EHR, ADT, case management, payer portal, and revenue cycle data triggered low-confidence escalation and suspended disposition.
Discharge transition completion was supported by source links, owner acceptance, downstream handoff, residual risk, reopen trigger, and audit trail.
Patient-safety signals triggered licensed review escalation while the lane limited itself to administrative blocker evidence.
Conflicting public, payer, and tenant policy was recorded and escalated instead of being synthesized into unsupported operational direction.
Tenant acute care terms were normalized to public baseline and tenant glossary with assumptions and missing-definition escalation.
Completion without source links, owner acceptance, downstream handoff, and audit trail was refused and the work remained held or reopened.
System access blockers held execution, documented retrieval gaps, routed remediation, and avoided unofficial gap filling.

notes: Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities.

The evaluation suite covers in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, documentation rework, PHI handling, clinical escalation, payer and utilization boundary refusal, coding and revenue-cycle routing, conflicting sources, discharge transition completion, patient safety, policy conflict, tenant terminology, completion evidence defects, and system access blockers.
Package remains explicit that runtime strategy is specification-based and does not claim retained implementation authority.
